questions.question: What simple criteria did Jesse Pollak outline for adding Farcaster validators as of late May 2026?
options: Run Snapchain, not be Neynar, and be in a geographical region different from the current validator set
Stake at least 32 ETH, run Geth, and operate in North America
Be an official Coinbase partner, run hub-nodejs, and have a verified Farcaster account
Run a custom fork of Snapchain, be outside the US, and pass a developer review
questions.explanation: In his post on May 27, 2026, Jesse stated that their criteria for adding validators right now is: run snapchain, not be neynar and be in a geographical region different from the current validator set.

questions.question: According to Jesse Pollak's May 27, 2026 post, what are the top two priorities in order for adding new validators?
questions.explanation: Jesse explicitly highlighted: 'top 2 priorities in order right now - geo and no majority.'

questions.question: When did the Neynar acquisition deal fully close according to Jesse Pollak's team timeline?
questions.explanation: Jesse's post mentions that 'the deal had fully closed at the beginning of april' in 2026.

questions.question: Why does the core team decline to onboard validators running modified or forked versions of Snapchain right now?
options: They are trying to avoid open-source codebase contributions
Modified clients require too much network bandwidth
A validator running a fork creates risk they don't have systems to verify from the outside and lack the dev capacity to take on
Forked clients violate the strict terms of the Neynar acquisition agreement
questions.explanation: Jesse explains that a validator running a fork creates risk they don't currently have systems set up to verify from the outside and don't have the dev capacity to take on right now.

questions.question: Jesse Pollak noted they merged PR contributions from which community developer, while clarifying that merged PRs do not guarantee 1:1 compatibility?
questions.explanation: Jesse's post says, 'yes, we've merged contributions that came through PRs from outside the core team, including from cassie.'

questions.question: In early May 2026, what did Jesse Pollak test moving to a different continent to pave the way for geo-decentralized validators?
questions.explanation: In his May 1, 2026 post, Jesse stated: 'this week - we're testing moving one our testnet nodes to a different continent.'

questions.question: Which FIP (Farcaster Improvement Proposal) did Jesse Pollak seek reviews on in late April 2026?
questions.explanation: On April 23, 2026, Jesse posted: 'Looking for feedback or reviews on the Snapchain Signers FIP'.

questions.question: What did Jesse Pollak claim in the Gnomie Ascii Sanctuary on April 21, 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse posted: 'I claimed Scruffy Duskhollow in the Gnomie Ascii Sanctuary! 🌲'

questions.question: On April 11, 2026, which shard on the Snapchain mainnet halted, prompting active resolution efforts from the team?
questions.explanation: Jesse posted on April 11, 2026: 'snapchain mainnet shard 1 has halted at ~32M height.'

questions.question: For his planned appearance at /frontiertower in April 2026, how did Jesse Pollak describe his planned outfit and appearance?
questions.explanation: Jesse's April 9, 2026 post states: 'I'll be the one with the whale face, orange t-shirt.'

questions.question: In March 2026, Jesse reported that wallet balances were showing up inaccurately due to backend changes. What was the specific known edge case still in progress?
options: Tokens with super low liquidity USDC pairs were not priced correctly
Ethereum balances showing up as zero
Optimism mainnet gas limits showing negative numbers
Farcaster storage units not syncing with wallets
questions.explanation: On March 13, 2026, Jesse posted: '1 known edge case fix that's in progress. > tokens with super low liquidity USDC pair' are not priced correctly.'

questions.question: Which major sports tournament final did Jesse Pollak celebrate watching with his kids in March 2026?
questions.explanation: On March 9, 2026, Jesse posted: 'Had so much fun watching the cricket World Cup final yesterday 🇮🇳🏆'.

questions.question: Following the cricket World Cup final, what did Jesse's kids discover about New Zealand's national team colors?
questions.explanation: Jesse wrote: 'They now know New Zealand is a team and their colors are black.'

questions.question: On February 24, 2026, Jesse Pollak warned of expected instability on the Farcaster network layer around 12:00 PM UTC due to what event?
options: Shard 0 cutting over to the updated validator set
An unexpected fiber optic cable failure
The launch of Neynar Studio web client
questions.explanation: Jesse's post on Feb 24, 2026 mentions: 'expecting some additional instability around 12:00 PM UTC on Feb 24 when shard 0 cuts over to the updated validator set.'

questions.question: When Base Official posted on March 15, 2024, what milestone did they highlight before sharing a blog link?
options: They reached 1 million transactions per day
They doubled their revenue last week
They hired a new product designer
questions.explanation: Base Official posted: 'last week we doubled our revenue and I have no idea why...'

questions.question: A November 2022 post from Base Official linked to a piece comparing early media coverage of the internet revolution to what?
questions.explanation: The post shared a link to a piece on 'how early media coverage of the internet revolution compares to that of crypto reporting of today'.

questions.question: What did Brian Armstrong ask Neynar for an API to check in March 2026?

Whether a user has a certain amount of USDC
Whether a user has a verified Farcaster badge

questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong posted: 'thanks for the beep @bigeon ask me anything for $0.25'.

He was moving to New Zealand

questions.question: Who is 'on a mission to send bugs right to jail!' according to Jesse Pollak's Neynar-themed post in February 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse posted: '@grin is on a mission to send bugs right to jail!... This is the Neynar way 🪐'.

questions.question: According to Jesse Pollak, which step is NOT part of the 'Neynar way' to handle bugs?
questions.explanation: The 5 steps listed are: 1. Detailed linear ticket 2. magic 3. test + review 4. ship 5. reply back to the bug reporter. Post a bounty is not one of them.

questions.question: What farm did Jesse Pollak's kids ask for in February 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse posted: 'My kids are now asking for their own /fruitling-valley farms'.

questions.question: Where did Jesse Pollak pull all-nighters playing games during grad school?
questions.explanation: Jesse posted: 'In grad school, there were days I pulled all nighters playing DoTA or LoL with friends at USC’s GamePipe Lab.'

questions.question: Jesse compared modern multiplayer software building with coding agents to what highly rewarding activity?
questions.explanation: Jesse wrote: 'Coding agents have shortened the time span of the effort reward cycle enough for it to feel as rewarding as a game today.'

questions.question: Jesse Pollak announced backend Farcaster database layer changes on Feb 5, 2026, during what specific time window?
questions.explanation: Jesse posted: 'we're making some significant changes at the database layer on Farcaster's backend today between noon - 1PM PST.'

questions.question: Jesse Pollak asked if anyone had given an OpenClaw agent access to which humanoid robot to move around their home?
questions.explanation: On Feb 3, 2026, Jesse posted: 'Has someone already given an OpenClaw agent access to a humanoid like the G1 to move around their home?'

questions.question: Who achieved '50% less global p95 latency' on Jan 30, 2026, according to Jesse's post?
questions.explanation: Jesse wrote: 'how it started yesterday vs. how its going today for @mcilroyc 50% less global p95 latency'.

questions.question: Which country's cricket account did Jesse Pollak praise on Jan 28, 2026, for bringing 'S tier shit posting' to the sport?
questions.explanation: Jesse posted: 'Cricket was missing S tier shit posting until Iceland showed up' with a link to @icelandcricket.

They deprecated the CLI in favor of Discord

questions.explanation: The post listed: secure configure of environment variables, browse files, and faster dev container availability, but did not mention an AI pair programmer chat.

questions.question: Jesse stated in January 2026 that 'tool call visibility' specifically helps them do what?
questions.explanation: Jesse wrote: 'tool call visibility helps us improve the bot faster'.

questions.explanation: Dan Romero posted on May 4, 2026: 'i’ve really fallen of if @v is higher on a poasting leaderboard'.

'Base is the home of AI agents'

questions.question: What did Brian Armstrong state he will NOT buy just because they are Pokémon themed?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong repeated three times on June 3, 2026: 'I will not buy SpaghettiOs just because they are Pokémon themed.'

questions.question: On May 27, 2026, Jesse reiterated that decentralized protocol progress requires prioritizing what above all else?
options: Stability and growth of the network
questions.explanation: Jesse noted: 'stability and growth of the network has to come first - a decentralized protocol that nobody uses doesn't serve anyone.'

questions.question: What was the status of the Snapchain network after the April 11, 2026 incident was resolved?
options: Shard 1 was back to producing blocks and the backlog of messages in the mempool was cleared
Shard 0 was merged with Shard 1 to handle traffic
Geth client was upgraded to prevent further halts
Shard 1 was permanently halted and replaced
questions.explanation: Jesse posted: 'network is back to being stable now. shard 1 is now producing blocks. backlog of messages in the mempool is cleared as well.'
